name: Arancine Siciliane (Stuffed Rice Balls)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Romano or Parmigiana cheese; grate
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix rice with beaten eggs, grated cheese, parsley, salt, and pepper.
2. Brown ground beef in a skillet and remove excess fat. Set aside.
3. Heat olive oil in another pan. Sauté onions until translucent.
4. Add the meat, tomato paste, and seasoning to the onions. Simmer for 15 minutes.
5. Stir in frozen peas and cook until peas are tender.
6. Let the filling mixture cool completely.
7. Mold about a quarter cup of rice mixture into your hand, making an indentation with your thumb.
8. Fill the indentation with one tablespoon of the cooled filling.
9. Cover the filling with another quarter cup of rice mixture and shape into a ball, ensuring the meat is securely sealed inside the rice.
10. Roll the ball in bread crumbs to coat evenly.
11. Heat oil in a deep-fat fryer or deep pan to 375°F (190°C).
12. Fry the balls until golden brown, then drain on paper towels.
13. Serve hot or at room temperature.
NOTE: Rice balls like these and a similar version stuffed with cheese are popular street snacks in Southern Italy and Sicily. The name "Arancine" comes from the word "orange," referring to the shape of the rice ball. For variations, try using risotto instead of plain rice, or experiment with different fillings such as vegetables and additional cheese.
